opening_angle: Connect intus's mission to serve vulnerable populations with Conner’s experience building HIPAA-aligned, operationally reliable security systems in high-stakes environments like the UC Office of the President
paragraph_focus: Direct alignment with Senior Cloud Security Engineer role—Azure focus, secure-by-default CI/CD, and first-security-engineer startup experience
Concrete evidence from UC role: secure cloud operations across Azure, IAM, secrets management, and containerized services
Demonstration of incident response and compliance-as-automation: HIPAA/SOC 2/HITRUST controls translated into deployable runbooks and remediation pipelines
Emphasis on operational reliability, documentation-first delivery, and building guardrails that scale across engineering teams
voice_controls: Use active verbs: built, operated, delivered, aligned, automated
Replace vague claims like 'deep expertise' with specific actions: 'implemented IAM policies for Azure workloads', 'built CSPM correlation logic using Zeek and Grafana'
Avoid passive constructions—always name the actor (I built, I designed, I owned)
Use JD keywords directly: secure-by-default, remediation workflows, operational reliability, compliance-as-automation
claims_to_avoid: No 'passionate about' or mission-statement fluff without concrete backing
No hypothetical metrics—omit numbers unless explicitly provided
